description:
Parent epic: V3 E9: Memory and compaction

Source task 3/6 from MILESTONE_V3.md:

- Decide whether memory configuration lives under `agent` in V3 and which manual
  memory APIs belong in the public surface.

Epic closure criteria:
- memory and compaction have separate, crisp docs and tests
- compaction does not mutate the canonical transcript

comments.text: Recorded the V3 memory decision: memory configuration belongs in the agent contract because it describes continuity, not a generic lifecycle callback. The public Jidoka surface should remain declarative plus inspection/tracing; no separate remember/recall helpers for V3. Manual writes/deletes/searches stay with the configured memory store or lower-level runtime boundary. Verified with mix format --check-formatted, mix compile --warnings-as-errors, mix test, mix docs --warnings-as-errors, mix doctor --raise, and git diff --check.
